metrics.narrative: Jackson Lewis has no quantified savings, cycle-time, or headcount target on record. The qualitative value case is real, however: Melissa Geddie is building the business case around 'faster contract cycles and improved compliance' and 'better visibility and governance' as she stands up procurement as a formal function (SF Value_to_Customer__c, SF Business_Objectives__c, both tied to the 23 Mar 2026 planning conversation). Quantifying the cost of today's ad hoc redlining and missed contract-expiration tracking is the natural first discovery question once she re-engages.

champion.narrative: No champion exists; Melissa Geddie is logged as an Evaluator, not an advocate, and per SF Why_not_Zycus__c she 'has spoken with Zycus a number of times in her work history, but has never chosen us as VOC in any projects' - a personal track record this deal must overcome rather than assume goodwill from. She did react positively to Merlin's Teams-based PR intake on the 15 Jan 2026 intro call (SF Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c), the one seed of advocacy on record, but it has not been tested since and no internal introductions or document-sharing have occurred.

competition.narrative: Four named vendors, Coupa, GEP Worldwide, iValua, and JAGGAER, sit in Competitors__c as of the 23 Mar 2026 update when Melissa said she was 'already in talks with vendors' (SF Competitors__c; Next_Step_History__c). No discovery or demo has happened with Zycus, so there is no dated evidence ranking any one of the four ahead. The more material signal is that Melissa has evaluated Zycus at prior employers multiple times without ever selecting it (SF Why_not_Zycus__c), meaning the historical baseline favors a rival pattern, not Zycus. The wedge is Merlin's Teams-native intake, which she has already reacted well to.

identify_pain.narrative: Jackson Lewis has no formal procurement function today: per SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, procurement 'lives siloed across all departments on an ad-hoc basis,' there is 'no centralized contract repository,' no way to track contract expirations, and redlining is 'a back and forth game' with no continuity in vetting new contracts. Melissa Geddie, hired as the company's first true procurement leader, owns this pain and is using it to justify CLM as the entry point for a broader source-to-pay build-out (SF Compelling_Event__c, Business_Objectives__c). This pain traces to her 15 Jan 2026 intro call and has not been re-validated on a recent call.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have been discussed. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is blank and no SOW/MSA, procurement-channel, or InfoSec conversation is on record. This is normal at Qualified stage, but there is also no forcing function (contract expiry, mandate) documented yet to create early visibility once the evaluation restarts.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified or contacted (SF Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c = false). Melissa Geddie holds only the Evaluator role on the single OpportunityContactRole logged, with no demonstrated budget authority for a $90k, four-product purchase. Jackson Lewis's Andrew Charwat and Clifford Forrester exist as account-level contacts but have never been engaged on this opportunity. The move is to ask Melissa, once she replies, who signs off on a platform purchase of this size.

decision_process.narrative: Only two Avoma touchpoints exist for this deal: an unrecorded 15 Jan 2026 introduction and a 23 Mar 2026 sync with no captured notes or transcript, so the call sequence reveals no process detail. SF next-step history describes Melissa 'putting together a committee to evaluate solutions' as of 23 Mar 2026, but the committee was never confirmed and no discovery or demo was scheduled before the relationship went quiet. The process as known so far: informal vendor conversations -> planned committee formation -> discovery/demo (never reached).

decision_criteria.narrative: No formal evaluation criteria are documented (SF Business_Requirements__c is blank and Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c = false). The 23 Mar 2026 next-step note records Melissa 'already in talks with vendors' and forming a committee to evaluate CLM plus eInvoicing/AP automation, but the specific weighting or must-have requirements were never captured before contact stopped. Confirming written criteria should be the first ask when discovery resumes.

day_summary.overall: The only signal today was a significant close-date push on Jackson Lewis PC, sliding from June 19 to August 27 — over two months out. No emails or calls logged alongside it, so the record doesn't show what prompted the delay, but a shift this large usually reflects a stalled internal process on the buyer side rather than a minor scheduling tweak. Ball is effectively in our court to find out why and re-engage.

north_star_verdict.headline: 106 days since the last verified buyer contact, with only 51 days to a 27 Aug 2026 close and zero discovery or demo executed against four named competitors.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Hold as long-dated Pipeline (not Best Case/Commit) until Melissa Geddie re-engages and a real discovery/demo date is confirmed; the 27 Aug 2026 close is not credible on current evidence.

flags: MULTI-THREAD IMMEDIATELY UPON RE-ENGAGEMENT: Single-threaded to Melissa (Sr. Manager, Procurement) with no visibility into the evaluation committee, EB, DM, or cross-functional stakeholders (finance, legal, IT) who must approve a CLM + AP automation decision. The 23 Mar next step promised committee formation but we never met them. Upon re-engagement, demand introductions to at least three committee members, map who controls budget (likely the CFO or finance executive), and identify a champion with power and access. A procurement-only thread in a $90k, multi-product, AI-enabled deal is a loss waiting to happen.

RUN COMPETITIVE DISCOVERY BEFORE DEMO: Four competitors (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) are in play but we have zero intelligence on who has demoed, how the buyer ranks them, or what criteria they are using. Positioning Zycus's differentiation (Merlin AI, integrated CLM + Source-to-Pay) requires knowing the gaps the other vendors leave open. Upon re-engagement, before scheduling a demo, run a 30-minute discovery call to surface: (a) which vendors have already presented, (b) how the committee scored them and on what criteria, (c) what they liked and what fell short, and (d) whether budget and timeline are still live. Then tailor the demo to the gaps Zycus uniquely fills.

Buyer unresponsive for 95 days; promised committee formation and disco/demo never occurred; project may have deprioritized, budget pulled, or moved to another vendor without our knowledge

RE-ENGAGE the relevant stakeholder: 95 days dark with 62 days to close. The promised disco/demo from 23 Mar never happened and the buyer has not responded. The single highest-leverage move is for the deal owner to directly re-engage Melissa (email + LinkedIn + phone) this week to confirm if the evaluation is still active. If no reply within 7 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-to-executive check-in with a senior Jackson Lewis stakeholder (CFO or CIO from account contact list). If still no engagement, disqualify by end of July and stop investing.

MULTI-THREAD IMMEDIATELY UPON RE-ENGAGEMENT: Single-threaded to Melissa (Sr. Manager, Procurement) with no visibility into the evaluation committee, EB, DM, or cross-functional stakeholders (finance, legal, IT) who must approve a CLM + AP automation decision. The 23 Mar next step promised committee formation but we never met them. Upon re-engagement, demand introductions to at least three committee members, map who controls budget (likely the CFO or finance executive), and identify a champion with power and access. A procurement-only thread in a $90k, multi-product, AI-enabled deal is a loss waiting to happen.

RUN COMPETITIVE DISCOVERY BEFORE DEMO: Four competitors (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER) are in play but we have zero intelligence on who has demoed, how the buyer ranks them, or what criteria they are using. Positioning Zycus's differentiation (Merlin AI, integrated CLM + Source-to-Pay) requires knowing the gaps the other vendors leave open. Upon re-engagement, before scheduling a demo, run a 30-minute discovery call to surface: (a) which vendors have already presented, (b) how the committee scored them and on what criteria, (c) what they liked and what fell short, and (d) whether budget and timeline are still live. Then tailor the demo to the gaps Zycus uniquely fills.
